# ddd-toolkit — Full API Reference

> Zero-dependency Domain-Driven Design building blocks for TypeScript. ESM-only, Node >= 24. Errors are values (`Result`/`Option`), the domain is pure, ownership is enforced at the repository port.

All imports come from the package root:

```typescript
import {
  Result, Option, Some, None, match, UUID, uuidv7, randomUuidV4,
  Entity, Aggregate, ValueObject, BaseDomainEvent, WatchedList,
  BaseRepository, ScopedRepository, RepoScope, createPaginatedResult, DEFAULT_PAGINATION,
  IUnitOfWork, AppErrorException, httpStatusFromCode, DatabaseOperationError, InputParseError,
  EventCollector, InMemoryEventDispatcher, onEvent, isEventHandler, domainEventToOutboxRow,
} from 'ddd-toolkit';
```

---

## Primitives

### Result<T, E = string>

A success-or-failure container. Recoverable failures are returned, not thrown.

- `Result.ok<T, E>(value?): Result<T, E>` — success (value optional).
- `Result.fail<T, E>(error): Result<T, E>` — failure carrying an error.
- `Result.combine(results: Result<unknown>[]): Result<unknown>` — first failure, else `ok()`.
- `.getValue(): T` — unwrap; throws on a failure.
- `.getError(): E` — unwrap error; throws on a success.
- `.isSuccess: boolean`, `.isFailure: boolean`.

```typescript
const r = Result.ok(42);
if (r.isSuccess) r.getValue(); // 42
Result.combine([Result.ok(1), Result.fail('E')]).isFailure; // true
```

### Option<T> / Some<T> / None<T> / match

Explicit optional; use instead of `null`/`undefined`.

- `Option.some(value)`, `Option.none<T>()`, `Option.fromNullable(value)`.
- `.isSome()`, `.isNone()`, `.unwrap()` (throws on None), `.unwrapOr(d)`, `.unwrapOrElse(fn)`.
- `.map(fn)`, `.flatMap(fn)`, `.filter(pred)`, `.or(optb)`, `.orElse(fn)`, `.xor(optb)`, `.inspect(fn)`.
- `.toUndefined()`, `.toNull()`.
- `match(option, { Some: v => ..., None: () => ... })` — exhaustive pattern match.

```typescript
Option.fromNullable(maybe).map(x => x * 2).unwrapOr(0);
match(opt, { Some: v => `got ${v}`, None: () => 'empty' });
```

### UUID<T extends string | number>, uuidv7(), randomUuidV4()

- `new UUID(value?)` — wraps `value`, or generates a UUID v7 when omitted.
- `.value: T`, `.equals(other?): boolean` — value equality narrowed to the same subclass.
- Subclass for nominal id types: `class OrderId extends UUID<string> {}`.
- `uuidv7(): string` — time-ordered v7 (sorts by creation time).
- `randomUuidV4(): string` — random v4.

---

## Domain

### Entity<T> (abstract) / IEntity<T>

Identity-based domain object. Equality compares `_id`, not props.

- `protected constructor(props: T, id?: UUID<string | number>)`.
- `_id: UUID<string | number>`, `_props: T` (documented escape hatches).
- `.equals(other?): boolean`, `.get(key): T[key]` (falls back to `_id` for an absent `id`).
- `.toObject(): Record<string, unknown>` — unwraps VOs, UUIDs, nested entities and watched lists.
- `.clone(props?): Entity<T>` — structural copy keeping the same identity.

### Aggregate<T> (abstract) extends Entity<T> / IAggregate

Aggregate root that records domain events.

- `protected addEvent(event)`, `protected addEvents(events)`.
- `.domainEvents: IDomainEvent[]` (defensive copy), `.clearEvents()`.
- `.pullDomainEvents(): IDomainEvent[]` — returns and clears atomically.
- `.hasEvents(): boolean`, `.getEventCount(): number`.

### ValueObject<T> (abstract)

Immutable, structurally-compared value.

- `protected abstract validate(value: T): Result<T>` — validate/normalize.
- `static create(value): Result<VO>` — constructs only if `validate` succeeds.
- `.value: T` (frozen), `.equals(other): boolean` (deep via JSON for objects).

```typescript
class Email extends ValueObject<string> {
  protected validate(v: string): Result<string> {
    return v.includes('@') ? Result.ok(v.toLowerCase()) : Result.fail('EMAIL_INVALID');
  }
}
Email.create('a@b.com'); // Result<Email>
```

### BaseDomainEvent<T> (abstract) / IDomainEvent<T>

- `IDomainEvent<T>`: `{ eventType, dateOccurred, aggregateId, payload }`.
- `BaseDomainEvent` stamps `dateOccurred`; subclasses set `eventType`, `aggregateId`, `payload`.

### WatchedList<T> (abstract)

Tracks a collection delta against its initial snapshot.

- `abstract compareItems(a, b): boolean` — item identity.
- `.add(item): Result<void>`, `.remove(item): Result<void>`.
- `.getItems()`, `.getNewItems()`, `.getRemovedItems()`, `.hasChanges()`.
- `.find(pred): Option<T>`, `.exists(item)`, `.count()`, `.mapToObject(): unknown[]`.

---

## Application

### BaseRepository<T, TTransaction = unknown>

Repository port. Reads return `Option`, every method returns `Result` (no throwing across the boundary).

`create`, `update`, `delete`, `findById`, `findAll`, `findMany`, `findBy`, `exists`, `count`.

### ScopedRepository<T, TScope extends RepoScope, TTransaction = unknown>

Same surface, but every operation takes a `scope`. A wrong owner yields `Option.none()` (read) or a not-found `Result.fail` (write) — never a forbidden error, so existence never leaks.

### RepoScope / ScopeOf<K>

```typescript
type RepoScope =
  | { kind: 'user'; userId: UUID<string> }
  | { kind: 'org'; organizationId: UUID<string> }
  | { kind: 'user-in-org'; userId: UUID<string>; organizationId: UUID<string> };
```

- Factories: `RepoScope.user(id)`, `RepoScope.org(id)`, `RepoScope.userInOrg(userId, orgId)`.
- Guards: `RepoScope.isUser(s)`, `RepoScope.isOrg(s)`, `RepoScope.isUserInOrg(s)`.
- `ScopeOf<'user'>` extracts a single variant.

### Pagination

- `PaginationParams { page; limit }`, `PaginatedResult<T> { data; pagination { page, limit, total, totalPages, hasNextPage, hasPreviousPage } }`.
- `createPaginatedResult(data, params, total): PaginatedResult<T>`.
- `DEFAULT_PAGINATION = { page: 1, limit: 20 }`.

### IUnitOfWork<TTx = unknown>

- `startTransaction(cb)` — plain transaction.
- `run(cb)` — transaction that also flushes `EventCollector` events (e.g. into an outbox) before commit.

### Error taxonomy

- `ErrorCode` = `` `${UPPERCASE}${ErrorSuffix}` `` (e.g. `USER_NOT_FOUND`).
- Suffix → HTTP status: `_UNAUTHORIZED`/`_REQUIRED`→401, `_FORBIDDEN`→403, `_NOT_FOUND`→404, `_CONFLICT`/`_BLOCKED`→409, `_INVALID`→400, `_INTEGRITY_FAILED`→422, `_RATE_LIMITED`→429, `_PROVIDER_FAILURE`→502, `_UNAVAILABLE`→503, `_TIMEOUT`→504.
- `httpStatusFromCode(code): number`.
- `AppError<Code> { code, message, metadata? }`, `AppErrorException` (throwable form).
- `DatabaseOperationError(message, options)`, `InputParseError(message, options)` — preserve `cause`.

---

## Events

### EventCollector

`AsyncLocalStorage`-backed, request-scoped event buffer.

- `runWithContext(cb)` — run work inside a fresh context.
- `add(events)` — buffer one or many (warns via the out-of-context logger if no context).
- `drain(): IDomainEvent[]` — return and clear buffered events.
- `hasContext(): boolean`, `setOutOfContextLogger(fn | null)`.

### Dispatchers

- `IEventDispatcher` — `subscribe`, `unsubscribe`, `dispatch`, `dispatchAll`, `isSubscribed`, `getHandlerCount`, `clearHandlers`.
- `InMemoryEventDispatcher implements IEventDispatcher` — in-process; a failing/throwing handler is logged (via `setLogger(EventDispatcherLogger)`) and never aborts the others.
- `EventHandlerFn<T>`, `IEventHandler<T>`.

### onEvent / isEventHandler

DI-friendly handler factory.

```typescript
const handler = onEvent('user.created', (deps: Deps) => async (event) => { ... });
isEventHandler(handler(deps)); // true
```

### Outbox mapping

- `domainEventToOutboxRow(event, scope): OutboxRow` — maps a domain event to a CloudEvents-shaped row.
- `OutboxRow`, `OutboxMappingScope { source, organizationId?, aggregateType, traceparent? }`.

---

## Source

- Repository: https://github.com/axelhamil/ddd-toolkit
- License: MIT
- Runtime: Node >= 24, ESM only, zero runtime dependencies (`node:crypto`, `node:async_hooks` from stdlib)
- Build: tsdown (ESM, dts, sourcemap, minify, treeshake); types validated with publint + are-the-types-wrong
- Tests: Vitest 4, 326 tests, V8 coverage at 90% thresholds
